data.content:
The file is generated successfully but never added to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild evaluates globs at project load time — before targets run — so the generated file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `Compile` (and `FileWrites` for clean support) inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and FileWrites (for Clean support) -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file during the build (before `CoreCompile`), but it never adds that file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's default glob for `Compile` items is evaluated at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, and also add it to `FileWrites` so it gets cleaned properly.

Let me use the skill for this since it's available.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and FileWrites (for Clean support) -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly. Here's the diagnosis:

**Problem:** MSBuild evaluates `Compile` item globs at project load time, *before* any targets execute. The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the file to `obj/…/GeneratedInfo.cs` but never told the compiler about it — so it was invisible to Roslyn.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target to:
- `<Compile Include="..."/>` — includes the generated file in compilation
- `<FileWrites Include="..."/>` — ensures `dotnet clean` removes it
